title: Rinorea glabra

The classification of Rinorea glabra reflects its evolutionary relationships with other species within the Violaceae family.

intro: Rinorea glabra, also known as the smooth cat's claw vine, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Violaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea glabra include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Rinorea glabra is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or capsule, and is also used as an ingredient in traditional Southeast Asian folk remedies, such as tonics and infusions.
